mappings.rationale: Covers the historical development of major industries including textiles, steel, chemicals, and manufacturing.
Relevant to the principles underlying the aerospace, automotive, and other major manufacturing industries.
Directly relevant to the chemical industry and the processing industries including plastics, rubber, dyes, and pharmaceuticals.
Relevant to the chemical, plastics, rubber, and man-made fibre processing industries that rely on organic chemical processes.
Covers the materials science underlying the steel, textile, leather, plastics, and rubber industries.
Directly relevant to the pharmaceuticals industry, a major processing industry in the section.
Covers industrial research and development, the final topic in the section.
Relevant to the economic organization and market structures of major manufacturing and processing industries.
Covers the historical development of major industries and the economic forces shaping manufacturing and service sectors.
Relevant to principles of organization of work and production across major industries.
Relevant to the chemical, plastics, and pharmaceuticals processing industries and their molecular foundations.
Relevant to the merchandising and marketing of consumer goods and service industries.
Relevant to the merchandising and marketing of consumer goods produced by major industries.
Relevant to the clothing and footwear industry and the textile industry.
Relevant to the financial organization and management of major manufacturing and processing industries.
Relevant to the lumber industry and the paper industry's reliance on forest resources.
Relevant to the pharmaceuticals industry and the cosmetics and personal care industry.
Relevant to industrial research and development and the organization of new manufacturing ventures.
Relevant to the social obligations of major industries including environmental and labor practices.
Relevant to the environmental impact and regulation of major processing industries such as chemicals and petroleum.
Covers the historical development and organization of major American industries, relevant to the section's industrial overview.
